question.text: 'Options on Goods' differ from 'Options on Futures' in that they:

answer.text: Devolve into direct delivery of the physical commodity rather than into futures positions
Can be exercised at any time (American style)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Options on Goods devolve into direct delivery of the underlying commodity. Options on Futures devolve into futures positions, which may then lead to delivery or cash settlement. **The Trap:** Both are European-style. Both require premium. Options on Goods are NOT cash-settled — they specifically result in physical delivery. **Cross-Exam Context:** This distinction is unique to commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Options on Goods benefit farmers and FPOs directly — they can lock in selling prices with limited premium cost and receive/deliver the actual commodity.
